title: Taxation experts
description: Examine, analyze, and interpret accounting records to prepare financial statements, give advice, or audit and evaluate statements prepared by others. Install or advise on systems of recording costs or other financial and budgetary data. Examine budget estimates for completeness, accuracy, and conformance with procedures and regulations. Analyze budgeting and accounting reports. Determine tax liability or collect taxes from individuals or business firms according to prescribed laws and regulations. (Based on 4 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Examine, analyze, and evaluate financial and accounting records, including tax forms, for accuracy, completeness, and compliance with statutory provisions to prepare financial statements and determine tax liabilities.
Confer with taxpayers or their representatives to discuss issues, laws, and regulations related to tax returns, and provide advice on tax planning and compliance.
Notify taxpayers of discrepancies in their accounts or any required actions, such as refunds or additional payments.
Examine budget estimates, analyze reports, and review operating budgets for accuracy and conformance to regulations, advising on budget preparation and adjustments.
Collect taxes owed from individuals or businesses, and investigate claims regarding inability to pay by researching financial information.
Maintain detailed records of taxpayer interactions, audit findings, and financial assessments, reporting to management on financial activities and compliance.
Direct the execution of legal documentation processes, including assessments and garnishments, and supervise staff involved in filing and recording financial records.
Participate in informal appeals hearings related to contested tax cases and keep up to date with changes in tax codes and accounting practices.
